item.title: Global companies delay IPOs and slash dividends as Middle East conflict rattles markets
item.description: The Middle East conflict has significantly impacted global financial markets, affected logistics and hindered the supply of raw materials integral ​to a host of industries.

item.title: China expands digital yuan programme with 12 new bank operators
item.description: China's central bank has expanded its digital yuan programme by adding a ​dozen additional banks as operators, confirming a Reuters report ‌last month.

item.title: Wall Street sees multi-billion dollar debut for Lilly's obesity pill despite Novo lead
item.description: Eli Lilly's newly approved weight-loss pill is expected to generate billions in sales as soon as this year, with analysts betting it will quickly ​narrow Novo Nordisk's head start in the oral market.

item.title: Exclusive: Russian oil output cuts are unavoidable as drone attacks shrink exports
item.description: Russian oil output cuts are imminent because Ukraine's strikes on port infrastructure, pipelines and refineries have reduced export capability by 1 million barrels per day, or a ​fifth of total capacity, three industry sources said on Thursday.

item.title: Morning Bid: No Easter truce
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today

item.title: Goldman Sachs completes Innovator Capital acquisition, lifting ETF assets to $90 billion
item.description: Goldman Sachs said on Thursday it has completed the acquisition of active exchange-traded fund provider ​Innovator Capital Management, expanding the Wall Street ‌bank's presence in the fast-growing active ETF segment.

item.title: Tariff-struck companies exploring loans backed by refund claims​
item.description: Some tariff-whiplashed companies are exploring using refund claims as collateral for loans, in the latest example of creative financing arising from the complicated process ​of getting refunds from Donald Trump's now-overturned "Liberation Day" tariffs.

item.title: Focus: Netflix searches for franchises after losing out on Harry Potter
item.description: After losing its bid for Warner Bros Discovery’s rich trove of characters and stories, Netflix is forging ahead with the challenging work of building culture-defining franchises on its own.

item.title: Tesla's China-made EV sales rise for second straight quarter
item.description: Tesla's China-made electric vehicle sales rose for a second consecutive quarter, holding up despite ​intensifying competition as the U.S. automaker broadened its ‌focus beyond EVs.

item.title: Ahead of Greek social media ban, parents desperate to separate children from phones
item.description: Greek mother Georgia ​Efstathiou has tried everything to loosen the grip that social media has on her 14-year-old son: heart-to-heart talks; ‌internet-free time; confiscating his phone. Arguments flare as she fights the allure of his screen and its videos and messages.

item.title: France to announce measures to favour data centres in coming weeks, Finance Minister says
item.description: French Finance ​Minister Roland ‌Lescure said on Thursday ​his ​government expects to ⁠announce in ​the coming ​weeks new measures to encourage ​setting ​up data centres in ‌the ⁠country.

item.title: Wall St futures slide as Trump signals tougher Iran strikes, oil jumps 6%
item.description: Futures tracking Wall Street's main indexes slid ​on Thursday as U.S. President Donald Trump signaled more aggressive attacks on Iran, dampening expectations for an ‌end to the month-long war and sending crude prices up 6%.

item.title: China says it supports law-abiding transnational deals after reports of Meta deal review
item.description: The Chinese government supports companies ​with transnational operations ‌and technology cooperation deals according to needs and ​the law, ​commerce ministry spokesperson He Yadong ⁠said on Thursday.

item.title: Trump's fresh Iran threats give investors a risk-off reality check
item.description: President Donald Trump's threat to bomb Iran back to the Stone Age has sharply raised stakes in a war now ​in its fifth week and crushed investors' hopes for a swift end to a conflict that is squeezing oil supplies and fanning inflation.

item.title: Global central banks mostly on hold as war muddies economic outlook
item.description: Major central banks pointed to uncertainty linked to the war in the Middle East ‌as they largely kept interest rates steady in March, with concerns over higher inflation and weaker growth clouding the global economic outlook.

item.title: Central banks' inflation mood puzzle: more judgment than science
item.description: The world's central bankers may be attempting the impossible: to get into the psyche of business executives, labour unions and ordinary households in real time to understand how they are navigating their finances ​through yet another energy shock.

item.title: Tracking the fate of the dollar a year on from 'Liberation Day'
item.description: A year on from U.S. President Donald Trump's sweeping 'Liberation Day' tariffs, ​the dollar looks in much stronger shape with its safe-haven credentials reasserted in the ‌face of war in the Middle East.

item.title: Crisis contractor for OpenAI, Anthropic eyes a move to combat extremism
item.description: People who show violent extremist tendencies on ChatGPT will be directed to human and chatbot‑based deradicalisation support through a new tool ​in development in New Zealand, the people behind it said.

item.title: Singapore charges one more individual with AI chip fraud
item.description: Singapore prosecutors charged one more person with fraud on Thursday for making false representations to U.S. server supplier Dell Technologies , ​linking her to two other individuals charged with similar offences in ‌February last year.

item.title: Morning Bid: Prime-time disappointment
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Ankur Banerjee

item.title: Hopes dim for swift end to Iran war after Trump speech
item.description: Hopes for a swift end to the Middle East war faded on Thursday after U.S. President Donald Trump vowed more aggressive strikes on Iran, in an eagerly anticipated address that disappointed investors ​hoping for clearer signals of a way out.

item.title: Instant View: Investor reactions to Trump's speech on Iran war
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump told the nation in a televised speech on Wednesday night that the U.S. military had nearly completed the goals it had set out to accomplish in its war with Iran and that the conflict would soon be ending.

item.title: Australian job vacancies rise 2.7% in February quarter
item.description: Job vacancies in Australia rebounded to their highest in ​more than a year in ‌the February quarter, data showed on Thursday, led by gains in the ​construction, retail and accommodation ​sectors.

item.title: Currencies tread water as investors brace for Trump's Iran address
item.description: Major currencies held steady in thin trading on Thursday, as investors awaited a high-stakes televised ‌address from U.S. President Donald Trump that is expected to shed light on a possible ceasefire in the Gulf war.

item.title: Oil prices drop on hopes of US pullback from Iran war
item.description: Oil prices fell more than $1 early on Thursday as markets awaited an address to the nation ​by President Donald Trump that could signal a U.S. ‌pullback from the war in Iran.

item.title: Trump says US has plenty of jet fuel for Europe, market disagrees
item.description: President Donald Trump this week told countries struggling to get jet ​fuel due to Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz to buy from the U.S., but analysts say there is ‌a major problem with his advice: the U.S. can't cover the global shortfall.

item.title: Stocks, currencies on tenterhooks ahead of Trump address on Iran war
item.description: Stocks inched higher, the dollar softened and oil slipped on Thursday as investors held their collective breath ahead of a speech from U.S. President Donald Trump that could outline the end of ​the war in the Middle East and boost risk appetite.

item.title: Trump administration readies new tariffs on select drugmakers, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: The Trump administration is set to ​announce tariffs as soon as Thursday ‌on drugmakers that have not struck deals guaranteeing low prices in the U.S., Bloomberg News ​reported on Wednesday, citing people ​familiar with the plan.

item.title: Hedge funds face worst monthly drawdown in over four years, Goldman Sachs tells clients
item.description: Global hedge funds last month faced their worst monthly drawdowns since January ​2022, Goldman Sachs said in a client note on Wednesday, as market volatility triggered by the Iran war battered stocks and weighed on ‌the performance of the world's biggest money managers.

item.title: Auto industry group calls for scrapping US gas tax, adopting vehicle fee
item.description: The head of a group representing nearly all major automakers on Wednesday called for scrapping ​the U.S. government's 18.4-cent per gallon tax on gasoline and replacing it ‌with a vehicle fee to pay for road repairs.
